title: Permutation-invariant lap-snapshot specialist
summary: At a given race lap, the model is really seeing a set of up to 20 competitors whose interactions matter.
rationale: At a given race lap, the model is really seeing a set of up to 20 competitors whose interactions matter. Set-based models are designed for exactly this order-invariant structure and recent F1 multi-agent work also treats competitor interaction as a first-class signal.
implementation_hint: Build a lightweight DeepSets/Set-Transformer specialist over all rows sharing `Race_Year x LapNumber`: encode each driver row, pool the full lap snapshot into a context embedding, concatenate that context back to each row, and predict `PitNextLap`. If full set modeling is too heavy, approximate it with a learned pooled snapshot embedding from quantile-binned field features. Blend this specialist with the best tabular booster rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Higher-upside than another hand-built threshold feature because it can learn collective race-state patterns and relative positioning automatically.
risk: This is the highest-variance idea here: more engineering, more tuning, and more risk of overfitting if the sequence/snapshot split is not strictly race-held-out.
